commands.review_summary:
The PR adds opt-in AI-generated session titles after successful replies, stores `autoTitle`, exposes `derivedTitle` in session lists, updates Control UI display, and adds config/schema/runtime-auth support for the title model call.

PR surface: Source +342, Tests +270. Total +612 across 24 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR findings: the merge result shows `runtime.modelAuth.getRuntimeAuthForModel` forwarding selector fields, and `session-title-generator` joins full user messages before the title call. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - The remaining code blockers are narrow repairs that can be attempted without deciding the broader feature policy.

target.title: fix(sessions): auto-generate session titles via AI summarization
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes the session-title scanner so it reads complete JSONL transcript records instead of treating a fixed 8192-byte chunk as a complete prefix.
- Aligns background title generation with the canonical session model identity used by the chat reply path.
- Skips sessions that already have a stronger stored title before reading transcript content or calling the title model.
- Adds focused regression coverage for long first user records, runtime session model routing, and manual/subject-titled sessions.

Fixes #77165

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Session title generation must still trigger when the first user JSONL record is very long and later user turns satisfy `sessionTitle.turnsBeforeTitle`. It must use the same persisted session model identity as the chat reply path, and it must not disclose transcript excerpts to the background title model when a manual `displayName`, channel `subject`, or existing `autoTitle` already provides a stronger title.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** Users who start sessions with long prompts should still see useful generated session titles after enough turns, and users who manually name a session or get a channel subject should not have transcript excerpts sent to a background title request that cannot change the displayed title.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux x86_64, Node v22.22.0, current PR worktree after syncing with current `origin/main`. The browser proof used the real gateway-served Control UI on loopback port 19034 with an isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`. The successful model endpoint was a local OpenAI-compatible mock provider (`mock/mock-title`) because the available external ZTE provider returned 401 and the DeepSeek provider returned 402 insufficient balance in this environment.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```bash
# Start an isolated gateway serving the real Control UI with sessionTitle enabled.
TASK_WORKTREE=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-87643 \
EVIDENCE_DIR=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-87643-evidence \
bash /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-87643-evidence/start-round4-ui-mock-gateway.sh

# Open the real Control UI in Playwright, connect through GatewayBrowserClient,
# send one long first turn plus two later turns, then verify chat.history,
# durable autoTitle, sessions.list derivedTitle, and screenshot display.
TASK_WORKTREE=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-87643 \
EVIDENCE_DIR=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-87643-evidence \
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-dev-pr87643-ui-mock/.openclaw-dev \
OPENCLAW_UI_URL=http://127.0.0.1:19034/ \
OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=proof-token-87643-ui-mock \
OPENCLAW_PROOF_PROVIDER_NOTE="local OpenAI-compatible mock provider (mock/mock-title) used because external ZTE returned 401 and DeepSeek returned 402 in this environment" \
node /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-87643-evidence/run-round4-ui-proof.mjs

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/session-title-generator.test.ts
```
- **Evidence after fix:**
```text
Control UI: http://127.0.0.1:19034
agent model: mock/mock-title (thinking=off, fast=off)
http server listening ...
ready

round4-ui-proof.md:
- Control UI page opened: http://127.0.0.1:19034/
- Provider used for this proof: local OpenAI-compatible mock provider (mock/mock-title) used because external ZTE returned 401 and DeepSeek returned 402 in this environment
- sessionTitle config after patch: {"enabled":true,"turnsBeforeTitle":3,"maxChars":50}
- First user message chars: 11297
- Chat turns completed: 1:ok, 2:ok, 3:ok
- Durable autoTitle: Nebula Banana Notebook
- sessions.list derivedTitle: Nebula Banana Notebook

round4-ui-proof.json:
- historyContainsAssistantMarker: true for OK-FIRST, OK-SECOND, and OK-THIRD
- durableEntry.modelProvider/model: mock/mock-title
- sessionsListRow.derivedTitle: Nebula Banana Notebook
- sessionsListRow.status: done

mock-openai-compatible-provider-requests.jsonl:
- three streamed chat completions for the three Control UI chat turns
- one streamed title-generation completion returning Nebula Banana Notebook

session-title-generator.test.ts:
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  5 passed (5)

pnpm check:test-types:
Exit status: passed

pnpm lint --threads=8:
[oxlint:core] finished
[oxlint:extensions] finished
[oxlint:scripts] finished

git diff --check:
passed with no output
```
- **Observed result after fix:** A real browser opened the Control UI, connected through the page's `GatewayBrowserClient`, confirmed `sessionTitle.enabled`, created a session, sent one 11,297-character first user message plus two later turns, waited for all three agent runs, confirmed each assistant reply marker in `chat.history`, observed durable `autoTitle: Nebula Banana Notebook`, fetched `sessions.list` with `derivedTitle: Nebula Banana Notebook`, and captured a screenshot showing `Nebula Banana Notebook` in the Control UI recent session list. The focused unit regression test also proves long-first-record counting, canonical runtime session model routing, and no transcript/model call for manual or subject-titled sessions.
- **What was not tested:** The successful browser proof used a local OpenAI-compatible mock provider rather than a paid external provider because available ZTE and DeepSeek credentials failed with auth/billing errors. No live Mantis/Desktop proof was captured. The mock proof still exercises the real gateway, Control UI, WebSocket RPC client, chat send/wait/history path, title generator, durable session store, and `sessions.list` display path.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix

## Review findings addressed

- RF-001 / RF-004 / RF-009: Title generation now resolves the same canonical session model identity used by the chat reply path, so persisted `modelProvider` + `model` sessions no longer fall back to the agent default model during background title generation.
- RF-002 / RF-003 / RF-007 / RF-008: Added redacted real behavior proof through the real gateway-served Control UI, browser `GatewayBrowserClient`, WebSocket RPC, three `chat.send` turns, `chat.history`, durable `autoTitle`, `sessions.list`, and screenshot display.
- RF-005 / RF-010: Sessions with manual `displayName`, channel `subject`, or existing `autoTitle` now return before transcript candidate resolution, transcript reads, or model calls.
- RF-006: Compatibility, auth-provider, and privacy sign-off remain maintainer decisions for the broader feature. This update narrows the privacy surface for titled sessions, keeps `sessionTitle.enabled` explicit opt-in, and discloses the remaining external-provider/Mantis proof gap.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** `merge-risk: compatibility`, `merge-risk: auth-provider`, `merge-risk: security-boundary`, plus session-state and message-delivery surfaces in the diff.
- **Risk explanation:** The broader PR adds a default-off `sessionTitle` config surface, durable `autoTitle` metadata, and background title model calls. This update narrows when background title calls happen, uses the session's canonical selected model, and fixes the long-record scanner without changing the default-off product gate.
- **Why acceptable:** Generation still requires `sessionTitle.enabled: true`; manual `displayName`, channel `subject`, and existing `autoTitle` now prevent any title-model transcript read; the scanner uses an existing bounded transcript line stream; and real Control UI proof confirms the gateway/session-list display path. Maintainers still own final sign-off on the broader config/privacy/auth-provider boundaries.

## Maintainer-ready confidence

- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High for this contributor update: the current author-side P1 code blockers are fixed, real Control UI proof is supplied, and focused test, test typecheck, lint, and diff whitespace checks pass. The broader merge decision still needs maintainer sign-off for the existing config/privacy/auth-provider risk labels.
- **Patch quality notes:** The latest production changes are narrow: use the existing canonical session model resolver, return before transcript reads for already titled sessions, and keep complete-line transcript scanning for long JSONL records. The existing broad catches/default returns are intentional non-blocking behavior for a background title feature: failed title generation must not block chat replies, and missing config/session/transcript state leaves the session unchanged. Runtime contract risk is limited to the after-reply background title projection/persistence path; it does not change the model-consumed transcript source of truth before the assistant reply. The large-diff risk belongs to the broader existing PR; this repair commit only touches `session-title-generator.ts` and its focused test. No dependency or lockfile changes are included.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Real browser/gateway proof plus focused unit regression tests.
- Target test file: `src/auto-reply/reply/session-title-generator.test.ts`.
- Real proof artifacts recorded: `round4-ui-proof.md`, `round4-ui-proof.json`, `round4-ui-proof-browser-console.log`, `round4-ui-proof-session-list.png`, `mock-openai-compatible-provider-requests.jsonl`, and `openclaw-dev-gateway-round4-ui-mock-port19034.log`.
- Scenario locked in: Long first JSONL user records do not block later turns from reaching the title threshold; generated titles persist as `autoTitle`; `sessions.list` exposes the generated title to Control UI; title generation uses the persisted runtime session model; and manual/subject-titled sessions do not disclose transcript excerpts to a background title request.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: It covers the exact scanner defect, the model-routing blocker, the privacy blocker, and the user-visible Control UI display path without widening the feature beyond its existing explicit opt-in gate.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The title generator previously made threshold and routing decisions at the wrong boundaries: it treated arbitrary byte chunks as JSONL records, reconstructed title-model identity from partial fields instead of the canonical session model resolver, and only checked `autoTitle` before transcript reads even though `displayName` and `subject` are stronger displayed titles.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The scanner now consumes complete transcript lines, model routing now uses the same resolver as the session reply path, and titled sessions return before transcript reads or model calls. That addresses the observed long-first-record failure, the session-model mismatch, and the unnecessary transcript disclosure for already titled sessions at their source.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
